criteria: The agent has read Section 3 completely to understand the SynthLang and Wiki2023+ datasets, including their construction procedures, sizes, and the role of answer-position annotations.

The agent has read Section 4.1 completely to understand the preliminary setup: models used, Adam optimizer with lr=1e-5 and linear decay, 3000/1800 training steps, batch size of 256, and the dual training objectives for QA and document data.
The agent has read Section 4.2 completely to understand all four analyzed training recipes: standard AR, Denoising AR (D-AR), Sentence Shuffling, and Attention Dropout.
The agent has read Section 4.3 to understand the positional bias study setup including the document modulation procedure D^k that swaps sentence s_1 with sentence s_k.
The agent has read Section 4.4 to understand the evaluation on unmodulated Wiki2023+ with position-grouped EM and F1 scoring across six position groups.

The agent has read Appendix B to understand the QA generation and filtering pipeline for Wiki2023+, including sentence extraction, LLM-based QA generation, and sentence-BERT classifier-based filtering.

The agent has read Appendix C to understand the key hyperparameters: D-AR token replacement ratio of 0.2, Attn Drop rates of 0.5 (SynthLang) and 0.2 (Wiki2023+), max training length of 512 tokens, and inference settings (temperature=0.6, top-k=50, repetition_penalty=1.2).
The agent has read Table 1 to understand the main comparison of AR, Shuffle, Attn Drop, and D-AR methods on unmodulated Wiki2023+ with Llama-2 7B.
The agent has read Table 2 to understand the cross-model comparison (Llama-2 7B/13B/70B, Llama-3.1 8B, Zephyr 7B, Mistral 7B) using AR and D-AR on unmodulated Wiki2023+.
The agent has read Figure 4 and Figure 5 to understand the per-position EM accuracy trends for each training method and model on modulated and unmodulated Wiki2023+.
The plan includes constructing the SynthLang dataset with generated person names, 5 languages per person chosen from 363 candidates, and QA pairs using a fixed template.

The plan includes constructing the Wiki2023+ dataset by downloading Wikipedia 2023 articles, extracting summary sections, splitting film documents into 1785/100/500 train/val/test, and generating QA pairs per sentence.
The plan includes the QA filtering pipeline for Wiki2023+: human annotation of 500 samples, training a sentence-BERT classifier, and keeping the top 60% by predicted validity score.
The plan includes implementing all four training methods: standard AR, Denoising AR (D-AR), Sentence Shuffling, and Attention Dropout.
The plan includes mixed mini-batch sampling that randomly draws from both QA and document data within each batch of 256 samples.

The plan includes the positional modulation experiment design: creating D^k document variants by swapping s_1 with s_k for k in {1, 2, 3, 4, 5} across all articles, and evaluating the model's ability to answer about the moved sentence.

The plan includes position-grouped evaluation for unmodulated Wiki2023+: grouping QA pairs by answer sentence index into six groups (positions 1-5 and a sixth group for positions >= 6), computing EM and F1 per group.
The plan includes loading all studied LLMs from HuggingFace: Llama-2 7B/13B/70B Chat, Llama-3.1 8B Instruct, Zephyr-7B-beta, and Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.3.

The plan includes ablation experiments: D-AR without denoising (no loss on replaced positions), Yes/No question evaluation, combined regularization (D-AR + Shuffle + Attn Drop), and text augmentation with ChatGPT-rephrased documents.

The code constructs the SynthLang dataset: generates person names, randomly assigns 5 languages per person from 363 candidates, and formats documents as 'Ahmed Lopez's 1st language is Fante. His/Her 2nd language is Tausug...', with 2000 training persons and 500 each for validation and testing.
The code constructs SynthLang QA pairs using the fixed template 'What is [person]'s [ordinal] language?' with 10000 training questions, and 500 per position group for validation and testing.
The code loads Wikipedia articles from the 2023 category (5911 articles total) and extracts only the summary section of each article.

The code implements QA generation for Wiki2023+ by feeding each sentence individually to an LLM (e.g., ChatGPT) and generating question-answer pairs, with each pair annotated with the index of the source sentence.
The code implements QA filtering using a sentence-BERT classifier trained on 500 human-annotated samples (valid/invalid), keeping QA pairs in the top 60% by predicted validity score.

The code correctly splits Wiki2023+ film documents into 1785 training, 100 validation, and 500 test documents, training on all 2385 film documents but using QA labels only from the 1785 training split.
The QA training objective computes the average negative log-likelihood on answer tokens only given question context: -(1/|a|) * sum_k log P(a_k | q, a_{<k}).
The document training objective computes standard next-token prediction loss over all document tokens, with the document prompted by its title: -(1/|d|) * sum_k log P(d_hat_k | t, d_{<k}).
D-AR is implemented by randomly selecting 20% of input token positions and replacing those input tokens with random tokens, while the loss target remains the original token sequence.
The D-AR modified objective computes -(1/|d|) * sum_k log P(d_k | t, d_tilde_{<k}), where d_tilde_{<k} are the corrupted (randomly-replaced) input tokens.
Sentence Shuffling is implemented by randomly permuting the sentence order within each document before computing the standard AR loss.
Attention Dropout is implemented by randomly dropping values in the causal attention mask during training, with dropout rate 0.5 for SynthLang and 0.2 for Wiki2023+.
Mixed mini-batch sampling draws 256 total samples per batch, randomly mixing QA data and document data, with separate loss objectives applied to each type.

The positional modulation procedure is implemented to create D^k versions of every document by swapping sentence s_1 with sentence s_k, for k in {1, 2, 3, 4, 5}, and training a separate model on each modulated dataset.

All HuggingFace instruction-tuned LLMs are loaded correctly: Llama-2 7B/13B/70B Chat (meta-llama/Llama-2-*b-chat-hf), Llama-3.1 8B Instruct (meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct), Zephyr-7B-beta (HuggingFaceH4/zephyr-7b-beta), and Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.3 (mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.3).
The optimizer is Adam with an initial learning rate of 1e-5 and linear decay scheduling, trained for 3000 steps on Wiki2023+ and 1800 steps on SynthLang.
The maximum training sequence length is set to 512 tokens.
Distributed training is implemented using DeepSpeed ZeRO3 to support efficient training on 8 A100 GPUs.
Text generation at inference uses temperature=0.6, top-k=50, and repetition_penalty=1.2 in the HuggingFace text generation pipeline.
Evaluation computes exact match (EM) and F1 scores per position group: positions 1 through 5 individually and a sixth group aggregating all positions >= 6 for unmodulated Wiki2023+.
The perplexity analysis computes the model's perplexity on the first sentence s_1 of the original document for models trained on each D^k variant, to measure position-dependent token dependence.
Attention entropy is computed per head and layer and averaged across positions, comparing AR and D-AR to quantify how much each model relies on prior tokens.

The Yes/No question variant of SynthLang is implemented by converting factual statements into binary questions (20000 training, 1000 val, 1000 test), generating one 'yes' and one 'no' question per original factual statement.

The D-AR denoising ablation is implemented by disabling loss computation on token positions where the input was replaced with a random token, isolating the contribution of the denoising objective from the noise perturbation.

The text augmentation experiment is implemented by rephrasing Wiki2023+ documents with ChatGPT in four ways (diversifying sentence order) and combining these augmented documents with originals during training.
Combined regularization experiments are implemented by applying D-AR together with Shuffle and/or Attn Drop simultaneously during training.

The code for the positional bias study on modulated Wiki2023+ (D^k for k=1..5) and SynthLang with all four models (Llama-2 7B, Llama-3.1 8B, Zephyr 7B, Mistral 7B) using AR training has been executed successfully.
The code for all four training methods (AR, D-AR, Shuffle, Attn Drop) on modulated Wiki2023+ with Llama-2 7B has been executed successfully for k=1..5.

The code for AR and D-AR training on unmodulated Wiki2023+ for all models (Llama-2 7B/13B/70B, Llama-3.1 8B, Zephyr 7B, Mistral 7B) has been executed successfully to produce Table 1 and Table 2 results.
The perplexity analysis on modulated Wiki2023+ documents (Fig. 6a) and the model-size perplexity comparison (Fig. 6b) have been executed successfully.
The Yes/No question experiment on SynthLang with Llama-2 7B and Mistral 7B using AR and D-AR training has been executed successfully.
The D-AR ablation experiment (no loss on replaced positions) on Wiki2023+ with Llama-2 7B has been executed successfully.
The combined regularization experiment (D-AR + Shuffle + Attn Drop combinations) on Wiki2023+ with Llama-2 7B has been executed successfully.
The text augmentation experiment with ChatGPT-rephrased documents combined with AR and D-AR on Wiki2023+ has been executed successfully.
The longer training experiment (doubled training iterations) for both AR and D-AR on Wiki2023+ with Llama-2 7B has been executed successfully.

The execution results for Figure 3 match: all studied LLMs (Llama-2 7B, Llama-3.1 8B, Zephyr 7B, Mistral 7B) show clear accuracy degradation as the answer position moves further from the first sentence in both Wiki2023+ and SynthLang when trained with AR.

The execution results for Figure 4 match: D-AR shows the highest EM accuracy across all positions and the smallest accuracy drop from position 1 to 5 compared to AR, Shuffle, and Attn Drop on Wiki2023+ with Llama-2 7B.

The execution results for Table 1 match: D-AR achieves the highest average EM (30.4) and F1 (55.3) over all six position groups, substantially outperforming AR (14.9 / 35.7), Shuffle (24.4 / 49.4), and Attn Drop (21.0 / 43.1) for Llama-2 7B on unmodulated Wiki2023+.
The execution results for Table 2 match: D-AR significantly improves over AR for all models, and Zephyr 7B and Mistral 7B achieve performance comparable to Llama-2 70B despite being smaller.

The execution results for Figure 5 match: D-AR significantly improves all studied models (Llama-2 70B, Llama-3.1 8B, Mistral 7B, Zephyr 7B) compared to AR across positions, and Llama-2 70B with D-AR greatly reduces positional performance degradation.

The perplexity analysis results match (Figure 6a): perplexity of s_1 increases as it is placed at later positions (D^k), and the AR model exhibits the steepest perplexity increase compared to D-AR and Shuffle; the trend mirrors the QA accuracy results.
The execution results for Figure 6b match: larger Llama-2 models (7B < 13B < 70B) show progressively smaller perplexity on s_1 when placed later, indicating greater robustness to positional bias.

The execution results for Figure 8 match: Yes/No question accuracy degrades as the answer position moves later in both Llama-2 7B and Mistral 7B; D-AR mitigates the degradation but the improvement is less pronounced than in standard QA experiments.

The D-AR ablation result matches: turning off the denoising loss on replaced tokens yields 29.3/54.8 EM/F1, only marginally lower than full D-AR (30.4/55.3), confirming that the noise perturbation rather than denoising is the main source of the performance gain.

The execution results for Table E match: adding ChatGPT-rephrased document augmentation improves both AR and D-AR performance, especially in early positions, and D-AR with augmentation (average EM 37.8) outperforms D-AR alone (30.4).

The execution results for Table F match: combining D-AR with Shuffle and/or Attn Drop further improves average EM and F1 over D-AR alone, with the full combination (D-AR + Shuffle + Attn Drop) achieving the best average EM (36.0) and F1 (68.0).
